page: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, delivering 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture with 22 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a bundled 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The server demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model inference for small and medium models, particularly during prompt processing and text generation. However, for large 70B models, text generation speed drops to the bottom 25%, highlighting a hardware limitation for very large model execution. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large block read and write operations perform in the middle 50%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the integration of dedicated GPU acceleration, substantial local NVMe SSD storage, and high-density AMD EPYC cores provides a consolidated hardware profile that reduces the need for external storage attachments. This server is optimized for workloads such as graphics-intensive applications, medium-scale LLM inference, database operations, software compilation, and ray tracing.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is an x86_64 server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a bundled 1880 GB NVMe SSD, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top 25% for multi-core CPU tasks, ray tracing, and database operations, while LLM inference for small and medium models reaches the top 10%. However, text generation for 70B models falls into the bottom 25%. This hardware configuration offers a cost-efficient, consolidated resource profile for graphics-intensive applications, medium-scale machine learning inference, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM, and 1880 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for graphics and medium LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, an NVIDIA L4 GPU, and 1880 GB NVMe storage for graphics and inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerated x86_64 platform combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA Ada Lovelace graphics architecture.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores at 3.4 GHz
NVIDIA L4 GPU with 22 GB VRAM for hardware-accelerated workloads
1880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top 10% performance in prompt processing and text generation for small and medium LLMs
Strong multi-core CPU, ray tracing, and database benchmark results
Suited for graphics rendering, medium-scale machine learning, and database operations
